title: Automation Protocol for Plasmid DNA Extraction from E. coli
author: Nina Alperovich

steps.instruction: Pellet cell cultures in 96-well deep-well plate by centrifugation at 3878 g (4500 rpm) for 10 minutes at 23 °C. ~1.8 mL culture per well (after cultures have been combined from growth plate)
Remove supernatant from the wells containing each cell pellet. Using 8-channel pipetting head Take care to avoid disturbing the cell pellet.
Resuspend each cell pellet in in 200 µL of Resuspension Buffer (P1); mix 10x by repeated aspiration and dispense. 8 samples at a time with the 8-channel head
Transfer resuspended cell samples to a new 96-well plate (Abgene, AB-1127) located on an automated microplate shaker. 8 samples at a time with the 8-channel head
Add 250 µL Lysate Buffer (P2) to each sample and mix by shaking the plate at 90 rpm for 2 minutes. 24 samples at a time with the 96-channel head
Add 350 µL cold (4 °C) Neutralization Buffer (N3, Qiagen, 19064) to each sample and mix by shaking at 90 rpm for 2 minutes. 24 samples at a time with the 96-channel head

Using wide bore tips (3.2 mm tip diameter), gently mixed the lysate samples by 3 repeated cycles of aspiration and dispensing, then transfer samples to a 96-well filter plate (Agilent, 201702-100) and allowed to settle in the filter plate for 2 minutes. 24 samples at a time with the 96-channel head
Use the filter press to push the lysate solutions through the filter plate into a new 96-well deep well plate (Eppendorf, 951033588) at 20 psi for 240 s followed by 65 psi for 60 s.

Transfer the cleared lysate solutions to a 96-well glass fiber binding plate (Nunc, 278010) and use the filter press to push the solutions through the binding plate at 40 psi for 60 s. 24 samples at a time with the 96-channel head
Add 900 µL Binding Buffer (PB, Qiagen, 19066) to each well. 24 samples at a time with the 96-channel head
Add 900 µL Wash Buffer (PE) to each well. 24 samples at a time with the 96-channel head
Use the filter press to push buffer through the binding plate at 40 psi for 60 s, then increase the pressure to 65 psi for 420 s to dry the binding plate.
Add 130 µL nuclease-free water (Thermo Scientific, AM9938) warmed to 60 °C to each well. 8 samples at a time with 8 channel head Jet dispense onto binding plate filters
Perform the final elution following the subsequent steps. Choose the step case appropriate for the type of equipment you have, either: a filter press or a centrifuge.
Use the filter press to push buffer through the binding plate at 40 psi for 60 s.
Elute DNA from binding plate into a 96-well low-binding elution plate (Eppendorf, 30603303) using the filter press at 65 psi for 7 minutes.

Carefully transfer the stacked elution plate, binding plate, and lid to a biosafety cabinet or other clean working space and gently remove the tape. Hold the stack firmly down against the inside surface of the biosafety cabinet or the bench top to make sure it does not jerk during removal of the tape.
Place the binding plate on top of the Elution plate (96-well low-binding plate 350 uL per well, Eppendorf, 30603303)
Start 5 minute timer.
Wait for 5 minutes
Place lid on binding plate.
Carefully move the stacked elution plate, binding plate, and lid from the liquid handler, and use tape to hold those three together in a stack:
Centrifuge the the stacked elution plate, binding plate, and lid at 1000 rpm for 3 minutes. Use an empty stack of similar labware for balance.
Remove the binding plate from the elution plate, and put the binding plate in an appropriate storage location if it will be used again or throw it to waste.
Seal the elution plate with a clear adhesive seal (Fisherbrand 8408240), and store at 4C until it is used for the BarSeq library prep.
Remove the binding plate from the elution plate, and put the binding plate in an appropriate storage location if it will be used again or throw it to waste.
Seal the elution plate with a clear adhesive seal (Fisherbrand 8408240), and store at 4C until it is used for the BarSeq library prep.
Put a lid on the elution plate, and move it to pickup location for user.
